name: Valley First
description_short: Valley First Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution offering checking, savings, loans, and credit cards with competitive rates and access to 30,000+ surcharge-free ATMs.
description_long:
Valley First Credit Union is a federally-chartered credit union serving members with a focus on community empowerment and financial well-being. The institution positions itself as more than a transactional lender, emphasizing personalized service and integrity. Their routing number is 321173522, and they maintain a physical presence with multiple branch locations, including their main J Street branch in Modesto.

Valley First offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business financial products. On the deposit side, they provide checking and savings accounts with no membership fee and a $1 starter deposit. They offer share certificates (12-month at 3.50% APY, with limited-time 4.00% APY certificates) and digital banking tools. Lending products include vehicle loans (from 5.49% APR), home loans (from 6.448% APR), personal loans (from 10.75% APR), credit cards (from 12.49% APR), and business credit cards (from 17.49% APR). Members also benefit from a debit card with 1% cash back and access to over 30,000 surcharge-free ATMs through the CO-OP Network.

Key differentiators include their member-focused approach, demonstrated through community initiatives like the inaugural Wes Hall Memorial Fund for youth programs and business support highlighted in their 2024 Impact Report. They explicitly emphasize fraud prevention and security, warning members they will never contact them via text or phone asking for password resets. The organization recently underwent federal charter conversion, signaling institutional stability and regulatory oversight. Their digital banking platform and mobile app provide convenience for account management.

As a credit union, Valley First operates on a not-for-profit model where member deposits fund lending and surplus returns to members through better rates and lower fees. However, potential members should note that access requires meeting membership eligibility requirements (which vary by location and affiliation), and their rates, while competitive, may vary based on individual creditworthiness. The organization serves a specific geographic footprint with branch locations, potentially limiting convenience for distant members despite ATM network access.
services: Checking accounts with no membership fee and $1 starter deposit
12-month share certificates (3.50% APY standard, 4.00% APY limited-time offer)
Credit cards from 12.49% APR with cash back rewards
Debit card with 1% cash back on purchases
Digital banking platform and mobile app
Surcharge-free ATM access through CO-OP Network (30,000+ locations)
Business services and small business lending
pros: Access to 30,000+ surcharge-free ATMs nationwide through CO-OP Network
Competitive rates: vehicle loans from 5.49% APR, home loans from 6.448% APR, personal loans from 10.75% APR
Limited-time certificate offer at 4.00% APY (standard 12-month at 3.50% APY)
1% cash back on debit card purchases with no annual fee to join
Strong fraud prevention measures with explicit communication about scam prevention
Community-focused with Wes Hall Memorial Fund grants to local youth programs
Comprehensive digital banking and mobile app for convenient account management
cons: Membership eligibility requirements may restrict access for some consumers (location/affiliation-based)
Physical branch network appears limited to specific California locations, reducing convenience for non-local members
Business credit card rates start at 17.49% APR, which is notably higher than consumer credit card rates
Certificate rates are modest compared to some online banks, with limited-time offers suggesting rates may decline
No mention of credit-building products (secured cards, credit builder loans) for members with limited credit history
best_for: Credit union members in California seeking competitive rates on auto loans, mortgages, and personal loans
Small business owners needing personalized lending and business banking services with community support
Consumers prioritizing fraud prevention, digital convenience, and surcharge-free ATM access nationwide
Savers looking for competitive certificate rates with periodic promotional APY offers
diagnosis: Valley First Credit Union is best for California residents and business owners seeking a member-owned alternative to traditional banks with competitive loan rates, strong fraud prevention, and nationwide ATM access. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility is restricted by location and affiliation requirements, and the physical branch network appears geographically limited, making it less suitable for those outside their service area without significant reliance on digital banking.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Valley First — Credit Union in Modesto, CA | CreditDoc
meta_description: Valley First — NCUA-insured credit union in Modesto, CA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
